findings.title: Explicit parallel batch for independent cold-load reads
findings.detail: On Activation instructs batching config files, memory-system.md, sidecar index (or init.md), style bible, course_context.yaml, and tool_policies.yaml in one round with no hard ordering dependencies. Reduces tool round-trips and latency versus sequential reads.

findings.title: Selective sidecar loading for patterns and chronology
findings.detail: index.md is primary on activation; patterns.md and chronology.md are load-when-needed. Avoids loading append-only history files on every activation, saving tokens on typical runs.

findings.title: Automated checks listed as sequential bullets
findings.detail: Step 1 invokes accessibility_checker.py then brand_validator.py as separate bullets with no guidance to run them concurrently. When both scripts only need artifact paths and disjoint outputs, parallel execution cuts wall-clock time roughly up to the slower script versus sum of both.
findings.action: Add explicit guidance to invoke accessibility_checker.py and brand_validator.py in parallel (single shell batch or concurrent tool calls) when inputs are ready, then merge results into the report.

findings.title: Sidecar entry point names index only; access-boundaries load implicit
findings.detail: references/memory-system.md requires access-boundaries.md on activation alongside index.md. SKILL.md only mandates the sidecar index as the entry point. If index omits a pointer to access-boundaries.md, the model may skip a short security file or reread memory-system.md for boundaries instead of the canonical sidecar file.
findings.action: In On Activation, add access-boundaries.md to the parallel batch when the sidecar exists (or state that index.md must always direct loading of access-boundaries.md first).

findings.title: Multi-artifact cross-checks described without parallel fan-out
findings.detail: Multi-artifact review pairs narration, slides, and invariants in one procedural section. For large sets of independent artifact pairs, a parent coordinator could delegate each pair to a subagent with a fixed JSON return shape to cap context growth; current text neither requires nor forbids this.
findings.action: For N+ paired artifacts, optionally document parallel subagent tasks per pair with ONLY-return JSON findings to keep parent context lean.

prepass_integration.notes: prepass reported empty sequential_patterns and issues; manual review covered SKILL, references, and memory strategy.

summary.assessment: Pre-pass was clean. The agent documents strong batched cold loads and selective memory; remaining gaps are optional parallel script execution, clarifying sidecar access-boundaries with index, and optional multi-artifact delegation for scale.
